preamble: Depalatalization of the voiced palatal stop [ɟ] to the voiced alveolar stop [d] is a process of consonant simplification and merger. As the palatal consonant series simplifies in language evolution, palatal stops often merge with their alveolar counterparts.

examples.note: Depalatalization of palatal stops to alveolars in various Slavic languages as palatal series simplifies or merges with alveolar series.
